mechanism_narrative: A lower FEDFUNDS rate transmits through discount-rate relief and easier financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when policy is not already at a tightening peak.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in a majority of months with falling FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below the inflation trend, real rates fall and bond-equity discounting weakens; this supports positive equity returns over the next 5 trading days only if inflation is not simultaneously surprising to the upside by a wide margin.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4% month over month and FEDFUNDS < 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or if CPIAUCSL acceleration ceases to improve short-horizon SPX returns after accounting for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and slower earnings growth, so higher unemployment should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month increases in UNRATE fail to associate with lower 20-day forward returns, or if the sign of the relation flips positive for any sustained 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp PAYEMS decline combined with a concurrent rise in UNRATE confirms an active labor-market deterioration shock, which should pressure forward equity returns through earnings downgrades and recession pricing over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls for 2 consecutive months and UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 pp over the same span
failure_condition: Reject if triggered windows do not show negative 60-day returns at least 60% of the time, or if the combined labor-shock signal has no incremental explanatory power beyond either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer demand and revenue growth for cyclicals, which should support positive S&P 500 performance over the next month when retail sales momentum broadens rather than concentrates in volatile one-month spikes.
failure_condition: Reject if positive month-over-month RSAFS changes do not correspond to above-baseline 20-day returns, or if the signal loses predictive power in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises after a prior contraction, it signals normalization in industrial supply capacity and better profit margins for manufacturers; this should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if sustained INDPRO recoveries are followed by negative 60-day returns more than half the time, or if the recovery effect disappears once revisions are accounted for.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in HOUST and rise in UNRATE identifies a housing-demand and labor-income shock that should weaken discretionary spending and future earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% year over year while UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint housing-labor trigger does not outperform either series alone and does not yield negative 20-day returns in at least 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A faster CPIAUCSL trend combined with a higher FEDFUNDS rate marks an active tightening regime in which inflation pressure dominates policy support, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as discount rates and margin pressure rise together.
failure_condition: Reject if tightening-regime triggers are not followed by negative 60-day returns in a majority of cases, or if the combined inflation-policy interaction has no edge over unconditional market direction.
